outcome_contract.why: When staff add products using the main upload workflow, products can appear live in the store without images, which looks unprofessional and loses sales â€” shoppers browsing a luxury fashion brand expect visuals for everything.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Products cannot go live without at least one image.

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: A: re-uses existing field-error channel without new UI components

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Block at button level; earlier feedback
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: A is consistent with existing form validation UX pattern

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: A is the right layer for action-level observability

stage_payload.decision_summary: The fact-find described a data integrity bug: products with no images could be persisted as `publishState: "live"` when a staff member confirmed the `would_unpublish` dialog. Analysis investigation reveals this is **not accurate** â€” `route.ts:286` already calls `deriveCatalogPublishState(productInput)` at persist time, which always returns `"draft"` when `imageFiles` is empty. The server was hardened in a prior plan (`xa-uploader-media-path-and-publish-flow-hardening`). The real remaining gap is UX-only: the client has no pre-save hard gate. Staff who set `publishState: "live"` with no images
goals: Add a hard client-side gate that blocks a "publish without images" save attempt with an inline error.
Preserve the `would_unpublish` confirmation flow as a server-side fallback (it is still useful as a last-resort guard).
Ensure TC-01 and TC-02 regression tests are written against the existing correct server behavior.
Ensure TC-03 tests the new client-side gate.
non_goals: Server-side changes â€” the server already normalizes `publishState` via `deriveCatalogPublishState`.
Image upload UI, R2 endpoint, or CDN changes.
constraints: The server's `deriveCatalogPublishState` normalization must NOT be removed â€” it is defense-in-depth.
assumptions: Staff can always save as "draft" (no images required for draft).
A live product with images being saved normally should never trigger the new gate.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Narrow is mandatory â€” only the save action should change
TC-03 (client gate) must be verifiable with the existing harness
Staff need an explicit message, not a confusing dialog for this case
Server normalization must remain intact regardless of client changes

options_considered.Description: Client-side hard gate in `handleSaveImpl`: check `publishState === "live" && imageFiles empty` before `doSave()`; set inline `imageFiles` field error and return `{ status: "error" }`
options_considered.Upside: Minimal â€” one check before the save attempt; testable with existing harness; no new files
options_considered.Key risks: None â€” the check uses already-available draft fields

options_considered.Description: UI-layer gate in `CatalogProductForm`: disable/error the Save button when `publishState === "live" && !hasImages`
options_considered.Upside: Earlier in the stack; gives visual feedback before user clicks Save
options_considered.Downside: Requires component-level testing; harder to write a precise TC; more coupling
options_considered.Key risks: May interact with autosave logic if autosave bypasses the form gate

options_considered.Description: Client gate (A) + rewrite `would_unpublish` dialog text to distinguish the two cases
options_considered.Downside: Additional string/i18n scope; dialog is already effectively dead-code for this path after A is added

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A â€” client-side hard gate in `handleSaveImpl`.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: Narrowest blast radius. One check in one function, testable with the existing harness, uses the already-available `setFieldErrors` callback and `splitList` utility. Option B provides earlier visual feedback but requires component-layer changes with heavier test infrastructure and creates an implicit dependency between form state and button affordance that the existing save action flow doesn't need. The server already handles the data integrity case; the fix needed is UX clarity, and Option A delivers that at the right abstraction layer.
chosen_approach.Option B (UI gate in form) â€” viable bu: changes the component layer unnecessarily when the action layer already has the right callback contract for field errors.
chosen_approach.Option C (dialog rewrite) â€” out of sco: the `would_unpublish` dialog will no longer fire for the "no images" case once Option A is in place, making the rewrite low-value and adds i18n surface.

rejected_approaches: Option B (UI gate in form) â€” viable but heavier: changes the component layer unnecessarily when the action layer already has the right callback contract for field errors.
Option C (dialog rewrite) â€” out of scope: the `would_unpublish` dialog will no longer fire for the "no images" case once Option A is in place, making the rewrite low-value and adds i18n surface.

sequencing_constraints: No external dependencies. TASK-01 and TASK-02 can be written together in a single commit.
risks_to_carry_forward: `splitList` import path in `catalogConsoleActions.ts` â€” needs to confirm the utility is accessible without new imports.
